result:
Done. All three operations are implemented in `CsvRow.calr`, the project builds cleanly, and every behavioral check passes — including the full `SplitRow(JoinRow(fields))` roundtrip on edge cases.

What I added to the `CsvRow` module (leaving `NeedsQuoting` untouched):

- **`EscapeField`** (`f002`) — returns the field verbatim unless `NeedsQuoting` holds, in which case it doubles internal quotes via `(replace field "\"" "\"\"")` and wraps the result in quotes. Pure (`§E{}`).
- **`JoinRow`** (`f003`) — folds over the list with a `first` flag, comma-separating the escaped fields; the empty list yields the empty string. Takes `List<str>` so the generated signature is `JoinRow(List<string>)` as pinned.
- **`SplitRow`** (`f004`) — a single-pass index scanner: a field starting with `"` is consumed until the un-doubled closing quote (doubled quotes contribute one literal quote, commas/CR/LF inside are content); otherwise it runs to the next comma or end of line. After each field it either skips the separator comma or ends, which naturally makes the empty line parse to `[""]` and a trailing comma produce a final empty field. Declares `§E{mut}` since it builds its result with `§PUSH`.

Two notes from the process: the reference sheet's `[str]` type maps to `string[]`, but the spec pins `List<string>`, so I confirmed in the Calor compiler's `TypeMapper` that the generic form `List<str>` emits `List<string>` and used that. I verified behavior with a throwaway console project outside the source tree (deleted afterward) — 22/22 checks passed, covering quoting, escaping, empty fields, trailing commas, and embedded commas/quotes/CRLF. That used 2 of the 10 build cycles; no test files were added and the project file is unmodified.
